summary: Frontend-only verification of the Split Sleeper Learn-tab shift identification feature. Logged in via Badge 042 / PIN 1234, navigated to /hours-of-service/split-sleeper, and verified every acceptance criterion listed in the review request. All shift markers, labels, colors, copy, and Practice-tab regression paths render as specified with zero runtime errors.

verification_matrix.shift_identification_card: PASS — [data-testid=shift-identification-card] rendered between legend and scenario cards. Contains 'How to identify the work shift', green START callout, red END callout, and italic 'Roadside rule of thumb' line. Copy includes 'first on-duty entry', '14 wall-clock hours', and 'LATER ... valid split pairing'.
verification_matrix.SL1: PASS — 1 start + 1 end. Labels: 'Shift start · 00:00 (first OD)' and 'Shift end · 20:00 (end of Period B)'.
verification_matrix.SL2: PASS — 1 start + 1 end. Labels: 'Shift start · 00:00 (first OD)' and 'Shift end · 21:00 (end of Period A)'.
verification_matrix.SL3_invalid: PASS — 1 start + 1 end. Labels: 'Shift start · 06:00 (first D)' and 'Shift end · 20:00 (14h wall-clock)'. Card text does NOT contain 'end of Period' wording.
verification_matrix.SL4_invalid: PASS — 1 start + 1 end. Labels: 'Shift start · 00:00 (first OD)' and 'Shift end · 14:00 (14h wall-clock)'. Description contains 'Any driving or on-duty work after 14:00 is a 14-hr violation'.
verification_matrix.SL5_day1: PASS — Day 1 grid has exactly 1 START marker at 06:00 and 0 END markers.
verification_matrix.SL5_day2: PASS — Day 2 grid has 1 END ('Day 1 shift ENDS · 03:00 (end of Period A)') and 1 START ('Day 2 shift STARTS · 03:00') whose pennant polygon fill is #2563EB (blue override, not green) as required.
verification_matrix.regression_cfr_links: PASS — 11 [data-testid=cfr-link] anchors rendered on the Learn tab; brackets + dashed vertical markers + pennant flags all render without errors.
verification_matrix.regression_practice_SP1: PASS — Practice tab loads, 2 selectable dashed rest blocks visible (matches SP1 qualifying indices 2 & 4). Clicking them + Submit showed selection-feedback; q-split rendered; selecting Yes → Next rendered q-hours (3-question flow intact).
verification_matrix.runtime_errors: None detected (no .error / [class*=error] nodes, no console errors).

critical_code_review_comments: EldGrid.js shift-marker data-testid uses sm.kind with fallback to index — since every shift marker in hosScenarios.js sets kind='start' or 'end', multiple markers on the same grid (e.g. SL5 Day 2) both end up with the same data-testid (shift-marker-start / shift-marker-end). This still works today because Playwright query_selector_all returns both, and the Day-2 markers have distinct kinds. But if a future scenario adds two 'start' markers on one grid they will share an identical testid. Consider appending the minute index (shift-marker-start-180) for uniqueness.

SL5 Day 2 has one 'start' and one 'end' marker at the SAME minute (03:00). Visually the two pennants overlap horizontally — they currently render one flag to the right of the dashed line so they coexist, but if design shifts the flag to the left it would collide. Keep the pennant offset in mind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Correct route is /hours-of-service/split-sleeper (NOT /hos/training/module-split — that path returns a 'No routes matched location' warning and a blank page). Review request's 'HoS → Training → module-split' refers to the in-app click path from the HosTrainingPage card whose id is 'split'. Login gate uses Badge then a separate PIN screen; selector input[type="password"] works for the PIN step. Selectable Practice rest blocks expose no data-testid on the <g> wrapper — use svg rect[stroke-dasharray="3 2"] to pick them.
